aboutPage.title: About Treevera
aboutPage.hint: Treevera is a website for exploring the taxonomic classification of living organisms. It organizes species into an interactive tree that goes from kingdoms down to species, with photos, descriptions, occurrence maps, and conservation status.

features.tree: Browse the full taxonomic tree, from kingdom to species, expanding each classification level.
features.species: View details for each species: photos, description, taxonomy, global occurrence map, and IUCN conservation status.
features.challenges: Play taxonomy challenges (daily, random, or custom) where you need to find a species by navigating the tree.
features.lists: Create and explore public species lists organized by theme, such as birds of Brazil, wild cats, or endangered species.
features.profile: Track your progress: species explored, challenges completed, accuracy, medals, and global ranking.

aboutPage.audience: Treevera is for anyone interested in biodiversity: biology students, teachers, researchers, or anyone curious about how living organisms are classified. It is developed independently and is constantly evolving.
aboutPage.sourcesHint: Click a source name to view its complete metadata.

paragraphs.occurrence: Occurrence data, maps, geographic statistics, and much of the taxonomic structure shown in Treevera are obtained from <gbif>GBIF</gbif>, which aggregates biodiversity records from institutions around the world.
paragraphs.conservation: For conservation status, Treevera consults the <iucn>IUCN Red List</iucn>, the main global reference for assessing species extinction risk.
paragraphs.photos: Species photographs are obtained from <inat>iNaturalist</inat>, a citizen science platform with millions of verified photographic records, and <commons>Wikimedia Commons</commons>, the Wikimedia Foundation's free media repository.
paragraphs.descriptions: Species and genus descriptions are obtained from <wikipedia>Wikipedia</wikipedia>. Additionally, Wikipedia and <gbif>GBIF</gbif> serve as alternative image sources when other sources do not return a photo for the requested species.

profilePage.rankingHint: Score on explored species, hit accuracy and speed to keep climbing.

tree.emptyNodeAcceptedSuffix: but it has no recorded descendants.

challenge.title: Daily Challenge
challenge.stepsTapHint: Click each step to see details
challenge.shareText: I'm playing {{speciesName}} on Treevera! Give it a try.
challenge.randomNoChallenge: No challenges available right now. Try again later.
challenge.incompatibleSpecies: This species has incompatible taxonomic data and cannot be used in challenges. It has been automatically removed from the species pool.
challenge.leaveWarning: You have a challenge in progress. Leaving now will cancel your progress. Do you want to continue?
challenge.noTips: No tips available for this step.
challenge.genusTip: First word of the scientific name of the species
challenge.followPathHelp: Expand the taxonomic tree groups following the correct path until you reach the species {{speciesName}}.
challenge.howToPlay1: The card shows the scientific name of the species you need to find.
challenge.howToPlay2: In the tree on the left, expand groups one level at a time: Kingdom → Phylum → Class → Order → Family → Genus → Species.
challenge.howToPlay3: Click the correct node at each level. Correct picks turn green; mistakes are counted but won't block your progress.
challenge.howToPlay4: Stuck? Open the hints (lightbulb icon). They highlight candidates in the tree and let you reveal the answer for the current step.
challenge.howToPlay5: Fill in all levels of the taxonomic path to complete the challenge.
challenge.customDescription: Create challenges using species you already know.
challenge.customNoChallengesHint: You haven't created any custom challenges yet.
challenge.infoDaily: A new species is released every day at midnight. In this mode, all players face the same species on the same day. You can also open the calendar to play challenges from previous days.
challenge.infoRandom: Play as many rounds as you want. Each new attempt picks a different species at random.
challenge.infoCustom: To create a challenge, open the menu on a species card (in your gallery or lists) or the species detail page and tap "Create challenge". It will appear under "My challenges" on this page.
challenge.customCreateDescription: This species will be used for a challenge exclusive to you.
challenge.customDeleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove the challenge with {{name}}?
challenge.customTaxonomyIncomplete: Incomplete taxonomic path. This species cannot be used in custom challenges.
challenge.customAlreadyExists: You already created a challenge with this species.
challenge.customLimitReached: 20-challenge limit reached. Remove one before creating another.
challenge.customError: Error creating challenge. Please try again.
challenge.shareResult:
I classified {{speciesName}} in {{time}} with {{accuracy}}% accuracy and {{errors}} error(s) on Treevera. Try it too!
{{url}}
challenge.statsDailyCompletedDesc: Total daily challenges you have completed.
challenge.statsStreakDesc: Consecutive days you completed at least one daily challenge. The streak resets if you skip a day.
challenge.statsAvgAccuracyDesc: Average accuracy across your daily and random challenges, calculated from your latest completed games.
challenge.statsSpeciesDiscoveredDesc: Number of unique species you encountered for the first time while playing challenges.
challenge.statsNoData: Play a challenge to track your progress.
challenge.statsAccuracyMin: Play at least 5 daily or random challenges to see your average accuracy.

settings.title: Settings

account.title: Account
account.saveError: Failed to save. Please try again.

tree.title: Taxonomic Tree
tree.description: Control how the taxonomic tree is displayed.
tree.showEmptyNodesDescription: By default, only nodes with descendants are shown. Enable this to show all nodes.
tree.showRankBadgeDescription: Always show the rank badge (for example: Order, Family) next to each node. When disabled, the badge only appears on hover.

audio.title: Audio
audio.audioDescription: Enable or disable sound effects during challenges.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.clearCacheDescription: Removes cached data such as species information you have already viewed. The data will be reloaded on the next request.

settings.loginToSavePreference: Sign in to save this preference.

gbif.label: GBIF
gbif.title: Global Biodiversity Information Facility
gbif.citation: GBIF. Global Biodiversity Information Facility. https://www.gbif.org/
gbif.description: Global open-data platform for biodiversity, bringing together species and occurrence records from around the world for research, conservation, and science.

iucn-red-list.label: IUCN Red List
iucn-red-list.title: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species
iucn-red-list.citation: IUCN. The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species. https://www.iucnredlist.org/
iucn-red-list.description: Global reference database on species extinction risk, with scientific assessments of the conservation status of fauna and flora.
iucn-red-list.provider: International Union for Conservation of Nature

inaturalist.label: iNaturalist
inaturalist.title: iNaturalist
inaturalist.description: Collaborative citizen science network where people record nature observations, identify species, and contribute to biodiversity monitoring.

wikimedia-commons.label: Wikimedia Commons
wikimedia-commons.title: Wikimedia Commons
wikimedia-commons.description: Free media repository maintained by the Wikimedia Foundation, with reusable images, audio, and video.

wikipedia.label: Wikipedia
wikipedia.title: Wikipedia
wikipedia.description: Free collaborative online encyclopedia, created and edited by volunteers, with articles in hundreds of languages on millions of topics.

vulnerability.unknown: Not in the IUCN Red List
vulnerability.unknownDesc: This species has no data in the IUCN Red List. The list assesses living species under current threats. Prehistoric species, geologically extinct groups, and poorly documented taxa often have no record.
vulnerability.EXDesc: This species has completely disappeared from Earth.
vulnerability.EWDesc: Survives only in captivity; extinct in its natural habitat.
vulnerability.CRDesc: Extremely high risk of extinction in the wild.
vulnerability.ENDesc: Very high risk of extinction in the wild.
vulnerability.VUDesc: High risk of extinction if threats persist.
vulnerability.NTDesc: Close to the threat threshold; closely monitored.
vulnerability.LCDesc: No significant threat of extinction at this time.
vulnerability.DDDesc: Insufficient data to assess the risk of extinction.
vulnerability.NEDesc: Not yet assessed under IUCN criteria.
